themes.title: Trump’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The attempted assassination of Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner has become a transnational rallying point for right-wing and far-right media, framing the incident as an attack on democracy and a symbol of escalating political violence. Coverage spans multiple countries, with shared narratives of security failures and calls for heightened vigilance against perceived left-wing threats.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Intense focus on security lapses, shooter's motives, and Trump's defiant response; articles highlight Secret Service failures and political rhetoric as contributing factors.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni expresses solidarity with Trump, framing the attack as part of a broader pattern of political violence; media emphasize the need for stronger security measures.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Coverage ties the incident to broader concerns about political extremism, with Bardella using it to criticize left-wing rhetoric in Europe.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage and others frame the attack as evidence of growing threats to conservative leaders, calling for stricter immigration controls and anti-extremism policies.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Abascal condemns the attack as an assault on democratic values, linking it to Spain's own political divisions and Vox's struggles with left-wing opposition.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD uses the incident to criticize mainstream media and left-wing parties, portraying Trump as a victim of political persecution.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish media emphasize the attack's implications for global democracy, with Nawrocki expressing solidarity with Trump and condemning political violence.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are experiencing a polling surge, with narratives emphasizing anti-immigration policies, national sovereignty, and opposition to EU fiscal rules. This trend is reflected in parallel coverage across multiple countries, often framed as a backlash against progressive governance and globalist policies.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads polls in multiple German states, with media emphasizing its anti-immigration stance and opposition to EU fiscal constraints.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Bardella's rising popularity is tied to market-friendly policies and opposition to EU migration policies, with media framing him as a viable 2027 presidential candidate.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox's polling gains are linked to its 'national priority' agenda, with media highlighting its influence in regional coalitions with PP.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD's growing influence in Swedish politics is framed as a challenge to traditional parties, with media emphasizing its role in shaping immigration and welfare policies.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP's polling surge is tied to its opposition to immigration and EU integration, with media emphasizing its potential to reshape Norway's political landscape.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK's rise is framed as a backlash against Labour and the Conservatives, with media emphasizing its anti-immigration and anti-EU policies.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega's push for fiscal flexibility and opposition to EU constraints is highlighted, with media framing it as a challenge to Meloni's leadership.

themes.title: PP-Vox coalitions spark constitutional debates
themes.blurb: The formation of coalitions between Spain's Partido Popular (PP) and Vox in regional governments has ignited debates over constitutional principles, particularly regarding immigration policies and national sovereignty. This theme resonates across multiple countries, with right-wing media framing these coalitions as either necessary for stability or a threat to democratic values.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox's 'national priority' policy in regional coalitions with PP is framed as a constitutional challenge, with media emphasizing its implications for immigration and social welfare.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Partido Popular
countries.note: PP's adoption of Vox's policies in coalitions is debated as a pragmatic move or a betrayal of constitutional values, with media highlighting internal divisions.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN's media coverage ties Spain's coalition debates to broader European trends, emphasizing the rise of far-right policies in governance.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian media frame Spain's coalitions as a cautionary tale, with Lega emphasizing the need for fiscal flexibility and national sovereignty in Italy's own coalition government.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish media highlight the constitutional implications of Spain's coalitions, framing them as part of a broader European shift toward right-wing governance.

themes.title: Orbán’s resignation reshapes Hungarian politics
themes.blurb: Viktor Orbán's resignation as Hungary's prime minister and his decision not to take a parliamentary seat have triggered a political earthquake, with far-right and right-wing media across Europe analyzing the implications for Hungary's future and the broader populist movement. Coverage emphasizes the rise of Péter Magyar's Tisza Party and the potential for democratic renewal.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Media focus on Orbán's resignation and the formation of a new parliamentary faction, with narratives emphasizing the need for renewal and opposition to Magyar's Tisza Party.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish media analyze Orbán's resignation as a cautionary tale for populist leaders, with coverage emphasizing the potential for democratic backsliding or renewal.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD's media frame Orbán's resignation as a sign of populist decline, while also highlighting the challenges facing Hungary's new government.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: EU-focused media emphasize the potential for Hungary to unlock frozen EU funds under Magyar's leadership, framing it as a test of Hungary's commitment to democratic values.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN's media tie Orbán's resignation to broader European trends, emphasizing the need for nationalist parties to adapt to changing political landscapes.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: The theme of far-right rhetoric contributing to political violence has gained traction in right-wing and far-right media, with coverage spanning multiple countries. Narratives often emphasize the dangers of left-wing extremism while downplaying far-right violence, framing it as a reaction to perceived threats to national identity and sovereignty.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Media highlight incidents of political violence tied to far-right rhetoric, often framing it as a response to left-wing extremism or immigration.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox's media emphasize the dangers of left-wing political violence, framing its own rhetoric as a necessary defense against threats to national identity.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage and others frame political violence as a consequence of left-wing extremism, calling for stricter measures to combat perceived threats to conservative values.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump-aligned media emphasize the dangers of left-wing political violence, often framing it as a direct threat to conservative leaders and values.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN's media tie political violence to immigration and left-wing extremism, framing far-right rhetoric as a necessary response to protect national identity.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish media highlight political violence as a consequence of left-wing extremism, emphasizing the need for stronger security measures and conservative leadership.
